obligatory prayer of and if there's imam we would say behind this imam and so on and so forth and we would give all these details everything except for our address and phone number and that is not correct there is no evidence of this that the prophet sallallahu alaihi wasallam ever did this not before not before salah not before fasting or zakah or any of the other worship there's only one instance and that is when a person begins his hajj or umrah and that's not really a pronunciation of one's it's not really a proclamation of one's intention as much as it is an initiation just we start our salah with allah we start our hajj with and so that is the first thing many people they feel kind of they don't want to differ with this they don't they want they don't want to leave it off they say okay i understand that the prophet salallahu didn't do it but what's the harm right what's the harm i will mention two things number one is i will prove to you that all that statement of pronouncing our intentions prior to prayer actually to us equals zero and i'll show you how let's say a person stands to make salah and in giving their pronouncing or proclaiming their intention they say no so they slip they misspeak instead of saying they say and then they start to pray they meant us but they said they misspoke is

something they're only speaking about their experience they're saying i've never seen it but it doesn't mean just because you haven't seen it it doesn't exist there are many things that we haven't seen that we haven't heard but they exist nonetheless and so the scholars of islam they tell us the one who affirms something they say i saw this happen we take their testimony ahead and we give precedence to that than the one who says i have not seen such and such ever happen it's because of that and this happens sometimes in the sunnah so ana as far as his experience goes he's never heard them say bismillah rahman or rahim at the beginning of doesn't mean that it never happened just means that he never experienced it he once said to his companions i'm going to pray for you exactly as i observe the prophet salallahu so that means that it happened he saw it but what we do understand from anas ibn malik is that the majority of the time that never happened the great majority very seldomly with the prophet sallallahu alaihi wasallam ever start his recitation with bismillah rather he would begin it with alhamdulillah we will now conclude for today for this evening next week insha allah we will discuss the way and what the prophet sallallahu alaihi foreign.

summary: This lecture is Part 8 of Zadul-Ma'ad (Provisions for the Hereafter), focusing on the physical mechanics and methodology of prayer (Salah) according to the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad. Sheikh Ali Mashhour begins by emphasizing the importance of patience in seeking knowledge and explains that while there are differences of opinion on prayer details, the core principles are agreed upon. The main content covers how the Prophet initiated prayer with Takbir (Allahu Akbar), the proper raising of hands to ear or shoulder level, the placement of the right hand over the left, and addresses misconceptions such as verbalizing intention before prayer, which has no authentic basis. The lecturer clarifies anatomical positions for hand placement and explains what is and is not permissible in prayer.
suggested_title: The Prophet's Method of Prayer: Hand Positions, Takbir, and Correcting Common Misconceptions
catchy_title: How Did the Prophet Actually Pray?
